commit.message:
feat(observability): expose session stats and completed runs (#37)

#### Context

The previous PR was already merged, while this branch adds new
observability work around session history, token cost estimates, and
review handoff cleanup.

#### TL;DR

*Track recent completed runs and expose session stats, model info, and
cost estimates in the observability APIs and dashboard.*

#### Summary

- Persist completed session records in the workspace and load them into
orchestrator snapshots
- Expose runtime version, configured model, completed runs, and cost
estimates in presenter payloads
- Add SessionStats unit tests and update observability/orchestrator
expectations for the richer payloads
- Remove the explicit `@codex` review request step from
`elixir/WORKFLOW.md` handoff guidance

#### Alternatives

- Keep the data only in logs, but that makes dashboards and API
consumers reconstruct session history themselves
- Expose only running-session metadata, but that leaves no lightweight
way to inspect recent completed runs

#### Test Plan

- [x] `make -C elixir all`
- [x] `cd elixir && mise exec -- mix test
test/symphony_elixir/extensions_test.exs
test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs
test/symphony_elixir/session_stats_test.exs`

---------

commit.message:
feat(workspace): add a repo-agnostic bootstrap template

Summary:
- add a generic Symphony bootstrap CLI and script for shared mirror +
  git worktree workspace creation
- derive per-repo cache directories from repo URL or explicit cache keys
  while keeping the BigClaw wrapper for compatibility
- update the BigClaw workflow, README, and template docs to use the
  repo-agnostic bootstrap contract

Rationale:
- make the local mirror/worktree flow reusable across repositories
  instead of tying it to BigClaw-specific script names and cache paths
- keep parallel Symphony workspaces from re-downloading the same repo
  on every issue by reusing one stable cache root per repository
- provide a copyable workflow template so downstream repos can adopt
  the same bootstrap pattern with only env defaults changed

commit.message:
fix(github): improve auth fallback handling (#35)

#### Context

Improve GitHub auth fallback behavior in the container runtime and align
the workflow guide with token-based GitHub access.

#### TL;DR

*Persist `gh` auth from token env vars and relax workflow blocker rules
around GitHub access.*

#### Summary

- Log `gh` into github.com from `GH_TOKEN` or `GITHUB_TOKEN` when
available
- Keep `gh auth setup-git` non-fatal and quiet after token-based login
- Add `api_key: $GITHUB_TOKEN` to the workflow tracker config
- Remove the hard prerequisite on GitHub MCP or `github_graphql`
- Update blocker guidance to require trying reasonable fallbacks first

#### Alternatives

- Keep env-only auth without `gh auth login`, but that leaves some
fallback flows unable to update GitHub state
- Keep GitHub as a special blocker case, but that conflicts with the
desired fallback-first workflow

- [ ] `make -C elixir all`
- [x] `git push -u origin HEAD`
- [x] Manual review of staged diff for `docker-entrypoint.sh` and
`elixir/WORKFLOW.md`
